channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wendy’s to offer Frosty customization with sauces, mix-Ins
item.description: Wendy’s will soon allow its customers to customize Frosty treats in what the company calls Frosty Swirls and Frosty Fusions. Unveiled during the company investor meeting on Thursday, Frosty Swirls will allow customers to add various sauces to a Classic Chocolate or Vanilla Frosty.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Mexican cartel leader’s son is sentenced to life in prison for role in major drug trafficking plot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Mexican drug cartel boss has been sentenced to life in prison for helping his father run one of the country’s largest and most violent narcotics trafficking organizations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ukraine supporters focus on hope and resilience as US relations sour and war carries on
item.description: The theme of “hope” was chosen long before this year’s deterioration of Washington-Kyiv relations, but participants at an international Ukrainian studies conference said that hope is needed more than ever — not only in Ukraine but in the United States itself.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Car being pulled from Columbia River might have belonged to Oregon family that vanished in 1958
item.description: A crane resumed dredging on the Columbia River on Friday as it tried to clear a way to pull out a station wagon that is believed to have belonged to an Oregon family of five who disappeared nearly 70 years ago while they were out searching for Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Lawsuit aims to strike down LGBTQ antidiscrimination protections in Pennsylvania
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Two public school districts and several parents have sued Pennsylvania in a bid to undo antidiscrimination protections for gay and transgender people, saying the state's two-year-old regulation is illegal because it goes beyond what lawmakers intended or allowed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Targeted Tesla attacks continue amid outrage at Elon Musk
item.description: Some Tesla showrooms, dealerships, and charging stations have been set on fire or vandalized in other ways, ostensibly in protest of Musk's role.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: States sue President Trump’s administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: Maryland and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies in a lawsuit alleging that President Donald Trump’s administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Daylight saving time is coming and the golf industry can't wait
item.description: Every year, bills are introduced in state legislatures across the country seeking to end the changing of clocks twice a year.
item.dc:creator: Margery A. Beck, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge stops compounded copies of Eli Lilly weight loss, diabetes drugs
item.description: Compounders had been allowed to produce and sell hundreds of thousands of their own versions of popular anti-obesity drugs, so long as the FDA considered them in shortage.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family on cruise ship gets lost luggage back after weeks of waiting, but half of their personal items are missing
item.description: Frustrations continue for Jason and Tammy Merritt after waiting a month for their lost luggage, which ended up being on-board the Carnival Cruise Line the entire time.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Elementary student found unharmed after escaping school
item.description: " I just jumped out of the car and tried to grab him to make sure that there was nobody that actually would hit him," Kohler said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at Indiana post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flight attendant pleads guilty to secretly recording a 14-year-old girl in plane bathroom
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A former American Airlines flight attendant has pleaded guilty to secretly recording video of a 14-year-old girl using an airplane bathroom and having recordings of four other girls using the lavatories.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gavin Newsom splits with Dems on trans athletes, prompting backlash from LGBTQ+ advocates
item.description: In a rebuke against the Democratic Party, California Gov. Gavin Newsom has revealed his thoughts on transgender athletes competing in girls' sporting events, and it has spurred reactions from across the state.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Walgreens set to go private in deal worth nearly $10 billion
item.description: Walgreens Boots Alliance says it has agreed to be acquired by private equity firm Sycamore Partners as the struggling retailer looks to turn itself around after years of losing money.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US foreign aid cuts leave a funding gap that private donors are unlikely to fill
item.description: Charitable organizations that relied on U.S. foreign assistance are looking to different private and public donors now that the Trump administration suspended nearly all foreign aid contracts. Past giving patterns suggest such a lifeline is unlikely.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Florida residents record SpaceX explosion
item.description: Residents in Tampa Bay, Florida were confounded after seeing what seemed to be an explosion in the sky around Thursday's sunset.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge says plans to release a woman in Slender Man case can go forward
item.description: WAUKESHA, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin woman who nearly killed her classmate years ago to please horror character Slender Man can be released from a psychiatric hospital as planned, a judge decided Thursday, rejecting state health officials’ last-minute attempt to keep her committed.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police stated that on February 22, troopers began investigating the theft of music equipment from a storage unit at D.M.D Mini Storage in Penn Township, Snyder County.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, KRON4

item.title: Tim Scott unveils bill targeting debanking
item.description: Debanking — the closure of accounts that banks consider risky, often with little notice or explanation — has become a key issue for Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Second measles death reported as outbreak grows in Southwest
item.description: The person was unvaccinated and a resident of Lea County, New Mexico, where at least 10 cases of the disease have been reported.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Tensions flare in House over Al Green censure
item.description: Tensions flared in the House on Thursday as lawmakers voted to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his disruptive protest during President Trump’s address to Congress, prompting a dramatic scene on the House floor — and demands from Republicans for further retribution.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Fire leads police to body found in clothing donation bin
item.description: Police are investigating a body found in a clothing donation bin in the parking lot of 70 Hudson Avenue. According to the Glens Falls Police Department, around 2:21 a.m., officers responded to an active fire at the donation bin and discovered the deceased person inside.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Abel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A 40-day Target boycott began this week. What to know about the protest and its potential impact
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A 40-day boycott of Target that calls for supporters to give up shopping at the company's stores during the Lenten period kicked off this week, to protest the discount retailer's decision to end some of its diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump Thursday paused the 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico that started earlier this week, with plans to restart them in April.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California company celebrates successful return of medical research space capsule
item.description: A Southern California company is celebrating the successful launch and return of a space capsule designed to change how pharmaceuticals are processed in Earth's low orbit. Varda Space Industries, based in El Segundo, launched its W-2 capsule aboard a Space X rocket in mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Pamela Bach, actor and ex-wife of David Hasselhoff, dies at 62
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Pamela Bach, an actor and the ex-wife of “Baywatch” star David Hasselhoff, has died at 62. The Los Angeles medical examiner's office reports that she died Wednesday and the cause is still under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bryan Kohberger doesn't want Amazon shopping list revealed at trial: court filings
item.description: Bryan Kohberger is asking a Boise, Idaho, judge to exclude his Amazon shopping list and 'click activity' from the University of Idaho student murders trial in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 skiers missing after avalanche hits remote mountain range in Alaska
item.description: Alaska State Troopers said three skiers are likely dead after an avalanche buried them in the Chugach Mountains near Girdwood, Alaska, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
